treatment: Retained HSE page titles, retrieval date, short source labels, UK occupational arboriculture scope, OGL v3.0 status, preferred acknowledgement, and adaptation notice.
reason: Required source identity, rights attribution, and scope.

treatment: Used the Crown-ownership-unless-indicated statement, OGL v3.0 link, preferred acknowledgement, image and multimedia caveat, and logo restriction only in provenance and the corpus licence notice. Excluded contact details and print-product boilerplate.
reason: Establishes eligible-text rights without importing legal boilerplate or assuming rights in every component.

treatment: Retained proper planning, appropriate supervision, safe conduct, and HSE's application of lifting-equipment concepts to arboriculture. Kept UK occupational context explicit.
reason: Planning and supervision are core system controls.

source_region: What is lifting equipment in arboriculture?
treatment: Retained that lifting/lowering equipment includes supporting, fixing, and anchoring attachments; a person is a load; and tree anchor points belong inside the lifting-system boundary. Excluded the long equipment example list.
reason: Defines the complete system boundary without endorsing devices.

treatment: Did not reproduce marking duties, maximum-load wording, rating values, or equipment lists. Retained only that a component marking cannot rate a tree or the complete system.
reason: Exact ratings and legal marking claims are outside the bounded synthesis and invite cross-domain misuse.

treatment: Retained diseased or decayed tree context, tree anchor as lifting-system component, and professional attention to species, age, condition, and disease. Excluded how to select an anchor.
reason: Tree context is essential, but the page does not supply a branch-capacity test.

treatment: Excluded mobile-platform, crane, counterweight, pole-tool, and access-machine recommendations and links.
reason: The corpus preserves the control hierarchy, not an endorsement of particular equipment.

source_region: Lifting people: daily inspection and rescue
treatment: Retained competent daily inspection of the main climbing rope and associated equipment and reliable site rescue with trained tree-rescue personnel. Excluded device and access-method instructions.
reason: Daily checking and rescue readiness are explicit HSE arboriculture safeguards.

source_region: Positioning: drift, fall, release, swing, and exclusion
treatment: Retained drift, free-fall, unintended-release, impact, other-worker, public, and pendulum-swing concerns plus supplementary-anchor context. Excluded placement and operating steps.
reason: Movement and consequence reasoning is useful without teaching a system configuration.

treatment: Excluded control-line attachment, climbing-above-anchor instruction, lowering friction systems, descent methods, named knots, connector gate type, and slinging procedure.
reason: These are operational climbing or rigging steps and specific-device prescriptions.

treatment: Retained competent thorough examination, trained daily pre-use checks, written weekly inspection records for high-wear ropes, defect notification, and record concepts. Excluded statutory timing detail.
reason: Separates daily checks, documented inspection, and thorough examination.

treatment: Did not reproduce regulation lists, legal links, PDFs, resource titles, or related-content navigation.
reason: Avoids legal claims outside the page scope and linked-publication trivia.

treatment: Retained that aerial tree work is hazardous and that lifting/lowering people or loads and work positioning sit within UK occupational work-at-height and lifting frameworks. Excluded incident-rate and legal-detail expansion.
reason: Defines source context without turning historical statistics into a risk estimate.

treatment: Retained HSE's minimum two-person aerial-work context and the need for an available, competent, equipped ground rescuer. Kept this within UK occupational treework.
reason: Rescue staffing is an explicit source requirement, not a recreational suspension standard.

treatment: Excluded chainsaw and PPE prescriptions, qualification-provider names, archived internal guidance, and linked third-party awarding bodies.
reason: External material was not reviewed or cleared and is outside lifting-system reasoning.

treatment: Retained pre-job planning with the climber, task awareness, possible dedicated ground staff on busy sites, continuous communication, observation, anticipation, public exclusion, and workload sharing.
reason: Ground staff are active system participants rather than passive observers.

treatment: Retained keeping lines free of knots, kinks, tangles, loose wood, machinery, vehicles, equipment, obstructions, and public contact, plus the warning never to wrap a working rope around the body for grip. Taught no hauling or friction method.
reason: Line housekeeping and unintended-body-anchor prevention are high-value, non-construction controls.

treatment: Retained continual assessment, modification of plan and risk assessment, and the explicit stop-and-reassess decision when unsure.
reason: This is the clearest changing-condition control in the source set.

source_region: Planning, risk assessment, equipment choice, and task-specific competence
treatment: Retained planned work, risk assessment, consideration of safer alternatives, suitable equipment, task-specific training, enough available people, and rescue competence. Avoided legal conclusions beyond the HSE page.
reason: Planning and competence determine whether a treework system is even considered.

treatment: Excluded changeover, footlocking, single-rope, ascent, descent, harness, friction-knot, and other technique examples.
reason: The corpus is not a climbing tutorial.

treatment: Retained two load-bearing anchors where possible, foreseeable-load framing, and the backup concept. Added common-mode and independence cautions; excluded anchor-selection and equipment-use steps.
reason: Anchor quantity alone does not establish independence or capacity.

source_region: Separately anchored working and safety lines
treatment: Retained distinct working- and safety-line roles, separate anchoring, and formal risk-assessed exception context. Excluded connection, ascent, descent, self-locking, mobile-device, and single-line operating instructions.
reason: Preserves redundancy reasoning without teaching a rope-access build.

source_region: ICoP, technical guide, video, research, and external resources
treatment: Did not access or use the video, industry code, technical guide, online learning, research PDF, chainsaw PDF, association site, archived report, or legislation pages.
reason: Linked content has separate authorship, rights, versions, and operational detail.

source_region: Planning, hierarchy, supervision, competence, inspection, and rescue
treatment: Retained avoiding work at height where practicable, then preventing falls, then reducing consequences; planning, organisation, supervision, competent people, suitable equipment, inspection, maintenance, emergency planning, and rescue.
reason: Provides a coherent control hierarchy around the treework pages.

source_region: Statistics, case studies, devices, and external law links
treatment: Excluded incident percentages, case studies, access-device examples, machine preference, regulation text, and general related links.
reason: Avoids stale risk estimates, device endorsements, and unreviewed legal material.

source_page: Thorough examinations and inspections of lifting equipment
treatment: Retained distinctions among pre-use check, interim inspection, maintenance, and systematic thorough examination; expected deterioration; exceptional events; and risk- and equipment-informed scope. Excluded general-industry examples and exact timing.
reason: Layered assurance is central to rope-equipment quality.

source_page: Thorough examinations and inspections of lifting equipment
treatment: Retained practical and theoretical knowledge, experience, defect detection, importance assessment, and sufficient independence from one's own maintenance work.
reason: Inspection competence is a judgment capability, not merely a role title.

source_page: Thorough examinations and inspections of lifting equipment
source_region: Statutory intervals, testing, enforcement, and linked standards
treatment: Excluded exact monthly and annual intervals, first-use and assembly deadlines, testing methods, overload discussion, statutory report fields, enforcement notifications, retention periods, British Standards, and linked PDFs or legislation.
reason: These legal and equipment-specific details exceed the bounded treework synthesis and may change.

source_page: Thorough examinations and inspections of lifting equipment
treatment: Retained traceable records, protection against unauthorized alteration, immediate communication of dangerous defects, withdrawal until risk is addressed, and condition trends. Excluded authority-reporting procedure.
reason: Defect control and traceability improve safety without reproducing legal forms.

source_page: Inspecting fall arrest equipment made from webbing or rope
treatment: Retained only that generic inspection principles for fall-arrest webbing can also apply to similar rope equipment. Did not fetch or use the linked leaflet.
reason: Supports inspection relevance while avoiding unreviewed procedures and thresholds.

treatment: Used the HSE hub only to verify current relationships among the overview, planning, lifting-person, and thorough-examination page families. Did not train on link or title recall.
reason: Routing provenance is useful, but navigation is not substantive content.

treatment: Recorded exact URL, retrieval time, HTTP status, content type, Last-Modified, raw byte length, raw SHA-256, normalized main-text length, word count, and digest for every page. Raw mixed-layout HTML was not retained or used directly for training.
reason: Page-level reproducibility without storing navigation, scripts, logos, or legal boilerplate.

source_region: Logos, images, SVG, videos, third-party and linked publications
treatment: Excluded the HSE logo, back-to-top SVG, site chrome, all images or multimedia, external training video, ICoP reader, association learning, qualification providers, legislation, standards, PDFs, archived material, and linked research. No linked component was fetched.
reason: HSE warns that images and multimedia may not be Crown-owned; linked works have separate rights and operational scope.
